text:
You are working on beads issue kapow-8ra: Production Mantine theme wiring (bug fix).

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/mantine-theme-fix"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## Issue Details
**kapow-8ra [BUG]: Production Mantine theme wiring**

Wire the shared Mantine theme into the production app. A custom theme exists and tests use it, but the live app mounts MantineProvider without the shared theme, creating a styling gap between test assumptions and production behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The production app mounts MantineProvider with the shared theme. Brand and accent token usages render correctly in live pages. Core page visuals are smoke-tested and existing tests continue to pass with the shared theme.

## Instructions
1. Read web/CLAUDE.md for frontend conventions
2. Read web/src/main.tsx — look at how MantineProvider is currently mounted
3. Read web/src/theme.ts — the shared theme definition
4. Read web/src/test/render.tsx — how tests use the theme
5. Fix main.tsx to pass the shared theme to MantineProvider:
   ```tsx
   import { theme } from './theme'
   // ...
   <MantineProvider theme={theme}>
   ```
6. Verify brand colors (brand.9, accent) are referenced in components and would now render correctly
7. Check if there are any existing tests that verify theme wiring — update if needed
8. Run all web tests and build to verify nothing breaks

## Quality Gate
Web-only change. Run: `cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test`

## When Done
Commit and push:
```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "fix: wire shared Mantine theme in production MantineProvider

Passes the shared theme to MantineProvider in main.tsx so brand and
accent colors render correctly in production, matching test assumptions.

Closes kapow-8ra

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
git push -u origin HEAD
```
Report completion with build/test results.
